zotero.abstractNote: We consider the problem of computing shortest paths on a manifold that is learned from data. This is a fundamental task in manifold learning, with applications in dimensionality reduction, data visualization, and generative modeling. Existing approaches typically rely on graph-based approximations, which are computationally expensive and sensitive to noise. We propose a novel approach that learns a Riemannian metric on the manifold, which allows us to compute shortest paths by solving a simple ordinary differential equation. Our approach is fast, robust to noise, and can be applied to high-dimensional data. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several synthetic and real-world datasets.
zotero.conferenceName: International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 22nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics
